channel.title: Downtown Bellefontaine
channel.description: Downtown Bellefontaine: Ohio’s Most Lovable Downtown!

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Welcome to downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio’s most loveable downtown located in Logan County. Why are we so loveable? Because we’re a community filled with dreamers, visionaries, and doers working to create big city feels in small town living. We’re shifting the perspective of small towns and putting our name on the map as we go. We’re more than a town, we’re a destination.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Welcome to downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio’s most loveable downtown located in Logan County. Why are we so loveable? Because we’re a community filled with dreamers, visionaries, and doers working to create big city feels in small town living. We’re shifting the perspective of small towns and putting our name on the map as we go. We’re more than a town, we’re a destination.

Welcome to downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio’s most loveable downtown located in Logan County. Why are we so loveable? Because we’re a community filled with dreamers, visionaries, and doers working to create big city feels in small town living. We’re shifting the perspective of small towns and putting our name on the map as we go. We’re more than a town, we’re a destination.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Our website address is: https://downtownbellefontaine.com.

What personal data we collect and why we collect it

Comments

When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Contact forms

Cookies

If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Analytics

Who we share your data with

How long we retain your data

If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where we send your data

Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

Your contact information

Additional information

How we protect your data

What data breach procedures we have in place

What third parties we receive data from

What automated decision making and/or profiling we do with user data

Industry regulatory disclosure requirements

item.title: Thank You
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Thank you for subscribing! Keep an eye on your inbox for upcoming events, giveaways, and everything happening in Downtown Bellefontaine.

item.title: Meeting Spaces
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
You’ve got business to take care of and we’ve got the venues to take care of you. Downtown Bellefontaine offers a variety of event spaces for whatever your event needs may be. From meetings to social gatherings to birthday parties, we cater to it all.

The Syndicate offers one of Logan County’s finest caterer and event centers. They can host your larger gatherings with their fine dining and venue space.

Bella Vino is a truly unique destination for your special event. Hidden away in the heart of Downtown, they provide you an old-world atmosphere with curated wine selections and catering options.  

The Maxwell is a premier event space located in the heart of Bellefontaine. It is the perfect venue for celebrating life's most important moments, whether you're hosting a wedding, corporate event, or social gathering. The Maxwell offers a stunning setting for unforgettable experiences.

Build Cowork + Space was designed to inspire, connect, and enable small businesses and solopreneurs. With four different styles of conference rooms available, there is a space to fit every meeting need. BUILD conference rooms also include the highest quality A/V equipment and next-level technology. Rent your space by the hour or reserve a room for the whole day. 

Putt and Play Golf Center is that perfect balance of work and play. Have your meeting then have some fun with their laser tag, miniature golf and golf stimulation, and virtual reality rooms. It’s also a great location for those birthday parties that deserves something extra special.

Make your next event one you won’t forget.

View more on our Downtown Map

item.title: MEDIA
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
2025

A central Ohio organization is setting the standard for small-town revitalization (Sept 23, 2025)
Pizza, beer and coffee are keys to success in Bellefontaine, one of Ohio’s best small towns (Sept 10, 2025)
From Ohio Pizza Shop to World Stage (Sept 10, 2025)
Bellefontaine is Ohio's Most Loveable Downtown (July 14, 2025)
'Small but mighty': How a central Ohio city became a blueprint for reviving America’s small towns (July 10, 2025)

2024

America's 'Oldest Concrete Street' Is A Historic Midwest Gem Near Cute Cafes And Shops (Dec 25, 2024)
Actors Noah Centineo and Will Poulter make Ohio trip (Dec 3, 2024)
Destination Shopping at the Christmas Capital of Ohio, Downtown Bellefontaine! (Nov 26, 2024)
Experience the Magic of The Holidays in Downtown Bellefontaine, an Enchanting Ohio Christmas Town (Oct 23, 2024)
7 Fun Things to Do in Bellefontaine, Ohio (Oct 7, 2024)
Thousands across the Miami Valley experience once-in-lifetime total solar eclipse (April 8, 2024)
Owner of Six Hundred Downtown in Bellefontaine talks about winning her sixth title at the World Pizza Games (April 2, 2024)
25 Unique Columbus Day Trips (March 15, 2024)
Rainbow Row Creates a New Bellefontaine Landmark (Jan/Feb, 2024)

2023

Rainbow Row project finalized in downtown Bellefontaine (Aug 21, 2023)
Economic Development: Private Investments Brings Big Change to Bellefontaine (June 29, 2023)

2022

Best Hometowns 2022: Bellefontaine (Nov 1, 2022)
DORA expands to seven days a week in Bellefontaine (July 13, 2022)
Take A Stroll Down Ohio’s Oldest Paved Road For A Picture Perfect Day (July 3, 2022)
The New Bellefontaine: Locals Bring New Life to Small Town Through Downtown Rejuvenation (April 29, 2022)

2021

Ohio developer Small Nation finds opportunity in state's small cities (Oct 22, 2021)
Bellefontaine Is A Shining Example of Urban Redevelopment (Sept 21, 2021)
Logan City Officials Tour "Revitalized" Ohio Town (Aug 26, 2021)

2020

A Small-Town Restaurant in Ohio, Six Hundred Downtown Serves Some of the Best Pizza Around (May 28, 2020)
Governor to Announce Reopen Date for Restaurants This Week, as the Advisory Board Wraps Up (May 4, 2020)
Many Small Businesses Say Loans Won't Get Them to Rehire (April 22, 2020)
Republicans Were Right About Unemployment's Perverse Incentives (April 21, 2020)
SBA Reports Data Breach in Disaster Loan Application Website (April 21, 2020)
Restaurants' Bailout Problem: Unemployment Pays More (April 20, 2020)
Lawmakers Fear Some Ohioans Don't Want to Work (April 16, 2020)
SBA Loans Helping Struggling Bellefontaine Businesses (April 15, 2020)
Recovery Group Discusses How to Reopen Ohio Businesses (April 15, 2020)
SBA Under Fire for Failing to Get Aid to Struggling Small Businesses (April 8, 2020)
After a Frenzied Rush to File for Small Business Loans, Entrepreneurs Nervously Wait on Bank Approvals (April 6, 2020)

2019

Why Facebook is Promoting Small Businesses with Big Downtown Columbus Advertisements (December 31, 2019)
Experience the Magic of the Holidays in Downtown Bellefontaine, an Enchanting Ohio Christmas Town (November 18, 2019)
Try Made-From-Scratch Donuts and Ice Cream at City Sweets & Creamery, Ohio's Drive-Thru Dessert Shop (October 5, 2019)
Sip Wine in a Private Underground Wine Cave at Bella Vino, a Cozy Event Space in Ohio (September 1, 2019)
This Historic 1950's Diner Has Been Renovated into an Incredible Ohio Restaurant (July 20, 2019)
Here's How You Can Spend the Perfect Summer Day in Ohio's Most Lovable Small Town (May 21, 2019)

item.title: DORA
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Established in 2020 for Special Events Only, the City of Bellefontaine's DORA is a designated public area where alcoholic beverages can be purchased in a designated cup from permitted establishments and carried within the district.

Where is Downtown Bellefontaine's DORA?

The DORA enhances Bellefontaine's revitalized downtown, encouraging more community members and visitors to stroll the area during special events. Keep up with Downtown Bellefontaine Ohio on Facebook to catch all of the upcoming events, and learn more about the City of Bellefontaine's Downtown DORA below!

In accordance with O.R.C. 4301.82 [B] [1] [b], the boundaries of the City of Bellefontaine DORA are shown below. This area contains 46.14 Acres.

Public Health and Safety

The City of Bellefontaine seeks to use a DORA District in its Historic Downtown for Special Events ONLY. All host entities for special events are required to develop and submit for approval a public health and safety plan along with their special event application. The proposed plan will be reviewed by city staff and others with interest along with the host entity. Only once the plan is agreed upon and all stipulations met will a permit be issued.

City staff will ensure that adequate sanitation, signage, and public safety requirements are met for each unique event. The necessity for portable bathrooms, handicap accessibility, pedestrian mobility, police and fire services, ingress and egress, crowd control, DORA boundary management and other factors will be addressed. Event organizers will be required to pay for special duty officers or overtime for public safety workers if necessary.

It is the City of Bellefontaine's intent that only beer and wine may be carried through the DORA District in City-approved DORA plastic cups.

The Chief of Police will dictate at DORA events the need for additional police officers at the expense of the host entity. The Chief of Police shall have the ability to revise or end a DORA event at any time if, in his/her professional opinion, it is in the public's best interest to do so.

DORA Rules

Enjoying the DORA safely and appropriately is very important, so there are a few rules to follow.​

  1. You must present your valid state or federal ID to a participating DORA vendor, be 21 years of age or older and receive a wristband that you must wear while carrying your beverage.
  2. You may purchase alcoholic beverages (beer and wine only) from a participating establishment or event vendor. No outside alcoholic beverages are permitted in the DORA.
  3. You may purchase up to two beverages at a time. Beverages must be in an approved DORA cup. 
  4. Any alcoholic beverages being consumed in public areas in the DORA must be in an approved DORA single use cup.
  5. You cannot carry a DORA beverage into any other DORA beverage selling business. You may carry your beverage into any non-alcohol serving business at the sole discretion of that business.
  6. DORA beverages may only be consumed during the DORA approved hours of operation. Possible hours for future DORA events include Monday through Saturday, 9 a.m. to midnight, and Sunday 12 p.m. to 9 p.m. 

DORA Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Can I walk anywhere with my DORA cup?
A: NO. Patrons can only carry DORA beverages in the defined area in the approved container. Retail and private establishments may allow DORA beverages or may opt to NOT allow them. Each establishment within the DORA will have their own policy.

Q: What is a DORA cup?
A: A plastic single-use cup designated for all establishments serving alcohol within the DORA. The DORA rules will be provided on a label with each cup.

Q: Can I take my DORA beverage into another establishment?
A: No. Once a DORA beverage has left the business where it was purchased, it must be consumed and the DORA cup disposed of prior to entering another establishment.

Q: What are the hours the DORA will be allowed to operate?
A: Generally, the allowable DORA event hours are Monday through Saturday, 9 a.m. to midnight. Sunday allowable hours are noon to 9 p.m. However, specific hours will be set for each DORA event. DORA beverages may not be consumed outside these hours.

Q: How will patrons know the DORA area limits?
A: Signs at key egress and endpoints will be installed on the boundaries. More information can be found on the DORA page on the City website.

Q: Can I pour my own alcohol in the DORA cup and carry it?
A: No. Beverages MUST be purchased from participating vendors. No cans, glass bottles, or outside beverages of any kind are allowed in public areas within DORA boundaries. In addition, DORA beverages may not be taken outside of the DORA route or patrons may be subject to legal consequences.

Q: Will the DORA area become littered, unruly, or noisy due to DORA?
A: Many cities have established DORAs and have had positive experiences. The City of Bellefontaine has multiple trash cans in the DORA area and may require the event sponsor to provide additional receptacles to keep the area clean. The Bellefontaine Police Department will be visibly present during DORA events and will be readily available should concerns arise. Special events may be required to provide additional police and public works staffing on an as needed basis.

item.title: ADVERTISE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Advertorial

This turn-key program produces all the assets needed to run an advertising editorial on the Downtown Bellefontaine website.

The advertorial includes:

  • Custom story written by a nationally-syndicated writer
  • Inclusion of 3-5 photos
  • Dedicated URL link to landing page
  • Facebook post on Downtown Bellefontaine page linking to article

Campaigns include website analytics for your advertorial as well as social media analytics.

Interested in advertising? Contact us today

item.dc:creator: admin_sn

item.title: Things to do
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Oh, the places you’ll go. Whether you’re looking for shopping, eating, or exploring, Bellefontaine offers you the whole package. But don’t take our word for it, come discover all we have in store for your next travel destination and see why we’re so loveable.  

Okay, you may not need our word, but you may need our advice. We’ve outlined some of the best itineraries that encompass all the must-sees of Logan County. Whether you’re attending a show at The Holland Theatre, heading to the ski slopes at Mad River Mountain, or soaking up the sunshine on Indian Lake, we’ve got the entertainment you need for any season.

There are hours’ worth of a good time in our small town. It’s a place tourists come to love and want to share with their friends. Let us give you something to talk about.

For more things to do, visit our Downtown Map.

While you’re here, be sure to check out all Logan County has to offer at www.experiencelogancounty.com

item.title: Places to shop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
We’re big on shopping small and shopping local and we love supporting the places that bring us to life. Our specialty shops are a huge contribution to the beauty in our downtown. Bellefontaine is your one-stop-shop to all things unique.

We’ve got gadgets and gismos of plenty to discover. Do you need some new clothes? The Poppy Seed’s got it. Any antiques to spruce up your home? Olde Mint Antique has it. Toys and games for the kids? Yep, The Fun Company has got that, too. The list goes on and on. There’s something for everyone so head over to our Downtown Map to find your perfect store.

So, whether you’re looking for those gifts to wow your friends and family or you’re treating yourself to those must-haves you can’t leave the store without, Bellefontaine is here for your ultimate shopping experience.

View more of our stores on our Downtown Map.

item.title: First Fridays
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
You gotta love a good Friday. And the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership, a nonprofit organization, has committed themselves to make every first Friday of the month one for the books. Their mission is to make Bellefontaine the place to be through improving the quality and quantity of commerce, unifying the public and private sectors, and promoting historic preservation to enrich the cultural life of our community.

How do they do it, you ask?

By hosting and promoting monthly downtown events that encourage people to come and support local shops in Logan County. Shop, eat, explore, repeat.

For more information, visit www.firstfridaysbellefontaine.com

item.title: Food & Drinks
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Around here, we treat everyone like a regular. Customer service is kind of our thing.

When you dine in Bellefontaine, you’ll visit restaurants that provide more than a meal; they’ll provide an experience. And not just any experience, but those experiences that you rave about for weeks after, ones that beg you to come back. Why? Because you feel right at home.  

Many of our downtown businesses were founded and run by Bellefontaine locals and their passion to keep this town the most loveable in Ohio is rooted in everything they do. Business owners like Ron and Kathy at Roundhouse Depot Brewing have been inspired by the revitalization that our downtown has seen and are very excited to continually keep Logan County strong. Come out to one of their events hosted at their brewery and get a taste of that love for local yourself.

So, what are you hungry for?

Doesn’t matter, Bellefontaine has it. We’ve got pizza at 600 Downtown, barbeque at 2Gs, burgers and craft beer at Iron City or The Syndicate, take your pick. We know you won’t be disappointed. But never forget dessert. Between City Sweets and Creamery and Whit’s Frozen Custard, we have options to satisfy every sweet tooth.

All that’s left is for you to taste your way across Bellefontaine and have that one-of-a-kind experience.

Check out our Downtown Map to help guide your foodie tour.

item.title: Events
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded: [vsel posts_per_page="5"]

item.title: Subscribe
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded: Stay up to date with the latest news, events, and happenings in Downtown Bellefontaine with the Downtown Newsletter!

item.title: Thank You
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded: Thank you for submitting your event! If approved, your event will show up on our website shortly.

item.title: Subscribe
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded:
Mission

The Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership, Inc. is dedicated to making the downtown attractive for merchants to do business and individuals to shop, while striving to improve the quality and quantity of commerce, unifying the public and private sectors, and promoting historic preservation to enrich the cultural life of our community.

Vision

Downtown Bellefontaine will be the Midwest's premier small-town destination for local food, unique shopping and meaningful experiences year around.

Executive Director
Katie Cooper
937-441-2681
kncooper9118@gmail.com

item.title: CONTACT US
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded: <!-- wp:gravityforms/form {"formId":"1","title":false,"description":false} /-->

item.title: Downtown Bellefontaine Spring Cleaning
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The 2025 Downtown Bellefontaine Spring Cleaning is Tuesday, April 22nd, and here's what you need to know:

Check in begins at 9:30 am at the Main Street Marketplace. We will start at 10 am!

You will receive your free t-shirt at check in - we would love for you to wear it for the day!

Free Six Hundred pizza and a free refreshment from Brewfontaine at lunch!

Weather is looking good! Be sure to watch and come dressed for the weather. We will have ponchos available just in case! If the weather is just misty or sprinkling, we will move forward! If it's pouring, we will cancel and you will receive email notification.

If you have any questions please email keyne@smallnation.com!

Thank you to our partners and sponsors:

item.title: QA event creation page
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded: [gravityform id="4" title="true"]

item.title: Historic Walking Tour
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Bellefontaine believes in preserving our city's rich history and sharing it with others. Here, the story of our small town is displayed through our proud, historic structures. Spanning architectural styles from the 1850s through brand new construction, our buildings tell the tales of our people, our dedication to growth, and our vision for the future.

As you walk through our downtown, look for the bronze plaques displayed on the building fronts. They'll introduce you to a small piece of Bellefontaine's history, while you meet our amazing present-day merchants and learn to love the most lovable town in the state!

Download Historic Walking Tour Map

Click on an image below for more information.

Interested in sponsoring a plaque for your building?

Inquire Here

item.title: Downtown Pumpkin Barrel Scavenger Hunt
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Explore Downtown Bellefontaine this Halloween season and discover the spooky Jack-o’-lantern pumpkin barrels! Each one has a unique name and story. Can you decipher these riddles and figure out which ones they’re talking about? Your mission is to visit each barrel, read its name and story, and determine which riddle is talking about it!

Can you match each riddle with its rightful pumpkin barrel?

Prizes

Turn in your completed scavenger hunt sheet to be entered into the raffle.
One entry per person

Grand Prize

One lucky winner will receive a $50 GIFT CARD to Six Hundred Downtown, Bellefontaine’s award-winning pizzeria!

Download Scavenger Hunt Sheet
Our Sponsors

item.title: Dev page1

item.content:encoded:

item.title: Walking Tour: Historic Post Office
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Dedicated in 1914, it was a "state of the art" Federal building of Neo-Classical style. The walls of the building are constructed of gray Ohio sandstone and the interior finished in marble and yellow pine. In 1963 the building was no longer able to serve the needs of the ever-changing postal service and in 1964 was replaced with a new post office on the opposite corner.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: H.M. Annat Dry Goods
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The building was erected in 1912 to house the new H.M. Annat Dry Goods which for many years was doing business in the Opera Block. The second floor of the store was used by the Deisel Wemmer Company for a branch cigar factory. The company became the R.G. Dunn cigar company and during it's peak employed 200-300 employees. In 1934 this building became a Montgomery Ward Store and continued to operate until 1986.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: Cozy Picture Palace
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
As the silent film era was beginning, H.J. King built this moving picture theatre in 1913. It was the first theatre in Bellefontaine built specifically for this purpose; it housed the Cozy Theatre and later the Rialto. The highest grossing film ever released was titled "Traffic in Souls." the film grossed approximately $450,000 and had a budget of only $5,700.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: Gorges Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Built by Henry Gorges in 1896 this building has since been known as the Gorges Building. The building is mostly remembered for housing pharmacies. The Frazer Drug Store was there as early as 1914 and remained for many years. The Gorges Building offers spectacular views of West Columbus Ave. and South Detroit Street. The second floor was a popular destination for rooms for rent for daily railroad travelers to Bellefontaine.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: Kauffman Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The entire block is known as the Kauffman Block, built by Max Kauffman in 1907. He operated the Syndicate Department Store at this location through 1918. Kennedy Brothers furniture replaced the Syndicate until it was sold and became Armstrong & Allen Furniture in 1927. Armstrong & Allen was in business for many years until 1990. In 1992, Canterbury Coffee opened for two decades.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: Dietrich Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
James Dietrich came to Bellefontaine in 1899 to establish his tailor business. Mr. Dietrich built two buildings at 114 and 116 N. Main. the first floors have been home of many businesses over the years. In the 1930s it was a jewelry store and from 1939-1954, it was Moore's Bakery then Winan's and Randall's Bakery respectively. The express purpose of the second floor was to serve as a lodge for the Elks.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: The Wissler Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
108 S. Main was rebuilt in 1856 after a devastating fire destroyed over two acres of downtown Bellefontaine. For a half-century until 1941, this building housed Wissler's Dry Goods. Over a year's time Clarence Wissler built an airplane in the second floor of this building when his parents owned it in the 1910s. Wissler successfully flew the airplane over Main Street in 1922. After the early 1940s, it became Uhlman's Department Store which remained in business for over 40 years.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: The J.C. Penney Co.
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Built after the fire of 1856, Churchill Hardware Co. operated at this location for over 70 years. The front of the building was remodeled in 1909. The J.C. Penney Co. occupied the building from 1927 to 1959, when it moved to the Zerbee Building at 118 S. Main and remained for more than 25 years. J.C. Penney himself attended the grand opening of the new location.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: The Grand Opera House and Opera Block
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The Grand Opera House's first performance was on December 23, 1880, featuring a presentation of "The Chimes of Normandy." The auditorium of the Opera House could seat 956 patrons. Many big-name acts performed at the Grand Opera House, including Buffalo Bill Cody, John Philip Sousa, Harry Houdini, and Jenny Lind, to name a few. The storefronts on both Court Avenue and South Main Street have been filled with many businesses over the last 140 years, including Hutchin's Dry Cleaners, White's Music House, and Richelieu Pure Foods.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: G.C. Murphy Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
130 S. Main, built in 1875, is the center section of what is known as the Buckeye Block. G.C. Murphy Co. was the most prominent tenant, occupying from 1917 to 1982. In 1956, the owners doubled the buildings' size to better accommodate the population of the city, and a growing number of customers. Many customers recall fond memories of the deli counter, soda fountain, and the aroma of hot roasted nuts permeating throughout the area.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: The Strand/Belle Theater
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The building was erected in 1884 and was converted into a movie theater in 1916 which was named The Strand. Sound equipment was installed in 1929 so that "talkies" could be shown. New equipment and air conditioning was added in 1950 and the name was changed to the Belle Theater. After the Belle closed, the building was occupied for many years as a Sears Roebuck Store. Most recently, the building was home to James Flooring for more than 20 years.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Walking Tour: The Canby Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Erected in 1912 by Edward Canby of Dayton, Ohio. It's developers intended the building to be very modern, and high-class structure for businesses. The Canby, as it was referred to, was orginally occupied by Morris & Palmer Dry Goods on the first floor. The second floor was occupied by Buckeye Cement Company. The third floor was home to an operations terminal of The Cleveland, Cincinnati, Chicago and St. Louis Railroad until 1939. At the time, the Canby was known as the handsomest building between Cincinnati and Cleveland.

item.title: Walking Tour: Shirk's Logan Tire Co.
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Built for Frank W. Shirk's Logan Tire Co. in 1928, which he owned and operated until 1956. Shirk's Logan Tire Co. served as the primary tire and car service company for Bellefontaine throughout the Great Depression and World War Two. This building sits across the street from the gorgeous Canby Building.

item.title: Walking Tour: The Patterson Building
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Built by William T. Patterson at the new site of his marble and monument works n 1912, which he began in 1875. After his passing in 1919, his son ran the business for decades after. Many businesses including a television store and stamp shop occupied this space since. The building still stands with its beautiful original marble.

Sponsored by:

item.title: Taste of Bellefontaine and Restaurant Week
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a week-long feast as we celebrate the culinary delights of Downtown Bellefontaine! Our local food and drink businesses are the heart and soul of our community, and we want to showcase their delicious offerings.

From July 10-16, our downtown restaurants and cafes will be serving up a special menu just for you. So, clear your schedule and get ready to indulge in some mouth-watering dishes. And don't forget to wear your stretch pants, because you won't want to miss out on any of the delectable treats!

SWEET AROMAS

$10 COFFEE FLIGHT
Coffee Flight featuring summer berries: Blueberry Cold Brew, Raspberry Chai, Blackberry and Satin Iced Latte and Strawberry Pink Drink.

FLYING PEPPER CANTINA

$25 MEAL DEAL
Ranch water (with alcohol or without)
Three Ground Beef Taquitos - featuring J&J Rice beef, topped with local lettuce and filled with a mix of local tomato, and onion and Humberto’s tres leche cake.

NATIVE COFFEE CO.

$10 PEACH BUNDLE
Pack of two peach macarons and a16oz Peaches and Cream Mocha.

SIX HUNDRED DOWNTOWN

$40 MEAL FOR TWO
Ten garlic knots, any medium specialty pizza, two cannolis, and two soft drinks. (Requires 2 vouchers).

CHEFHOUSE

$14 HOT CHICKEN
Cornbread pancake with Nashville Hot Chicken on a skewer with ranch and a pickle, and a side of street corn. (Located at the Logan County Fairgrounds.)

BREWFONTAINE

$25 MEAL DEAL
House made Roasted Red Pepper Bisque
Onion, garlic, roasted red peppers, smoked gouda, and parmesan cheese in a chicken and cream stock blended into a creamy bisque. Topped with shaved parmesan and house made croutons.
Wrap of Bellefontaine
Turkey, bacon, spinach, basil, and a house made sundried tomato and house made garlic, sundried tomato, basil cream cheese spread in a spinach tortilla.
Strawberry Cheesecake Cup
House made strawberry cheesecake filling layered with graham cracker crumbs and a strawberry coulis, topped with whipped topping and fresh strawberries.

THE SYNDICATE

$35 MEAL DEAL
CHOOSE 1 APPETIZER
Candied Black Pepper Bacon
Brown Sugar Candied, Cracked Pepper Smoked Bacon, Sweet Chili Garlic Aioli
Barbacoa Beef Empanadas
Roasted Jalapeno Crema, Pineapple Pico di Gallo
CHOOSE 1 ENTREE
Creamy Chicken Piccata
Egg Wash Chicken Breast, Lemon, Capers, Garlic, White Wine, Basil, Touch of Cream, Blistered Tomatoes, Egg Linguini
Citrus Honey Soy Glazed Grilled Salmon
Miso Hollandaise, Garlicky Ginger Broccolini, Pineapple Pico di Gallo
DESSERT
Blueberry Cheesecake Bread Pudding
Creme Anglaise-Soaked Blueberry Muffins, Croissants, Cream Cheese, Blueberries, Blueberry Compote, Whipped Cream

First Fridays are sponsored by:

A Taste of Bellefontaine is sponsored by:

Lori Newman & Malia Hughes.

Check out upcoming events at FirstFridaysBellefontaine.com.

item.title: Loft & Hidden Spaces Tour
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join your friends and neighbors on a fun historic walking tour of several historic buildings in Downtown Bellefontaine. Explore upper floor lofts and hidden spaces on Saturday, August 26th from 1:00 – 4:00 pm.

This self-guided tour will also include several newly redeveloped and completed upper floor spaces with breath-taking finishes, living and working spaces, and forgotten about hidden spaces throughout the downtown.

Purchase your $10 tickets in advance. Check in will take place inside the Main Street Marketplace. Limited tickets will be available at check in.

All proceeds raised will benefit the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership.

GET YOUR TICKET

item.title: Celebrate Christmas in Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This holiday season, there’s no better place to experience the magic of Christmas than in Downtown Bellefontaine. The streets are filled with festive cheer, twinkling lights, and one-of-a-kind shopping experiences that make our community the perfect destination for holiday fun.

Whether you're here to shop for the perfect gift, participate in our beloved traditions, or explore holiday events, Downtown Bellefontaine is ready to welcome you to Ohio’s Christmas Capital!

Click here to view our Downtown Bellefontaine Holiday Map and Shopping Guide!

Shop Local for the Perfect Christmas Gifts

Downtown Bellefontaine is home to a variety of unique local shops, offering the perfect opportunity to find special gifts for everyone on your holiday list. Whether you're shopping for children, friends, or family, our vibrant downtown has something for everyone.

For the little ones in your life, The Fun Company is the go-to destination for the best children's toys and games. With a wide selection of both classic and modern options, you can find everything from educational kits to puzzles and creative playsets that are sure to bring smiles on Christmas morning.

If you're looking for a unique, one-of-a-kind gift, be sure to visit the Nest 1896. This charming shop offers a wide range of handcrafted items and beautiful home décor that will make your presents 

For those who love to celebrate local pride, Four Acre Clothing Co. is the perfect spot to pick up Bellefontaine-themed apparel and accessories. Their stylish designs capture the spirit of our community, making their products ideal for anyone who wants to show off their hometown pride this Christmas.

Make sure explore all the amazing shops, restaurants, and experiences that Downtown Bellefontaine has to offer this holiday season with our downtown holiday guide. Whether you're looking to enjoy a delicious meal or find the perfect gift, our downtown businesses have you covered. When you're unsure of what to get, a gift card to one of your favorite local businesses is always a great choice, giving your loved ones the freedom to enjoy their favorite shops and restaurants at their convenience.

Visit Santa at the Santa Shed

A beloved tradition in Downtown Bellefontaine, the Santa Shed, presented by the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership, is a must-visit during the holiday season. Located in front of the historic Courthouse, the Santa Shed is the perfect place to bring the whole family for a magical meet and greet with Santa himself.

Santa will be available on Saturdays, Sundays, and select Fridays throughout the Christmas season to listen to holiday wishes, pose for photos, and spread plenty of Christmas cheer. Don’t miss the chance to create lasting holiday memories with your loved ones at this festive spot in the heart of downtown!

November 22 through December 21 
Saturdays: 1 PM – 4 PM
Sundays: 2 PM – 4 PM
Friday, December 12 & 19: 5 PM - 7 PM

Holiday Events in Downtown Bellefontaine

The holiday season is packed with festive events for the whole family. From the annual Christmas parade to special concerts and visits from Santa, there’s always something happening downtown.

November 14: Holiday Candle & Ornament Painting Night at Make Time
November 14-16: Holiday Kickoff Event at Peachtree Boutique
November 21-23: Holly Jolly Hometown Open House  
November 22: Downtown Santa Shed Grand Opening
November 22: Christmas Candle Workshop: Limited Edition Holiday Set at Make Time
November 28: Black Friday in Downtown Bellefontaine
November 29: Small Business Saturday in Downtown Bellefontaine
December 5: Hometown Christmas Parade
December 5: Coffee, Cocoa & Carols for a Cause at Native Coffee
December 6-7: Brunch with Santa at the Syndicate
December 6: Christmas Cookie Decorating at Make Time
December 7: Christmas Candle Workshop: Limited Edition Holiday Set at Make Time
December 7: It's a Wonderful Life: A Life Radio Play at the Holland Theatre
December 10-11: Brunch with Santa at the Syndicate
December 12-14: Downtown Bellefontaine Carriage Rides
December 13: The Drowsy Lads: An Irish Christmas at the Holland Theatre
December 14: Christmas in the Shire: A Cozy Craft Night at Allways Brewin'
December 16: West Central Ohio Community Concert Band Christmas Concert at the Holland Theatre
December 16: The Yuletide 5 at the Holland Theatre
December 20: Reindeer Meet & Mingle

Interactive Downtown Map

Planning your visit is easier than ever with our interactive downtown map. Use it to explore all the shopping, dining, and event locations, and get the most out of your holiday experience in Downtown Bellefontaine!

Click to View Interactive Map
